roadmap.name: RoadForge Public Alpha Readiness + GitHub Links

tasks.title: Audit large files and define refactor boundaries
tasks.desc:
Create a read-only map of current large frontend/backend files and where responsibilities are mixed.


**Scope**
- Identify files that act as orchestration hubs, especially Workspace, TaskRow, save/sync hooks, roadmap services, router modules, and CSS ownership.
- Mark what should be split now versus deferred until after alpha.
- Document invariants that must not break: local-first cache, whole-task lock safety, autosync, import/export compatibility, role gates.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] File responsibility map exists in docs/architecture or an implementation note.
- [ ] Each proposed split has a concrete target module and rollback-safe slice.
- [ ] No code changes in this audit slice.


**Validation**
`pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build` if docs imports or references are touched; otherwise review only.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Largely superseded: architecture docs (service split plan, source-of-truth rules, partial-write decision) and this Graphify audit already provide the map.
- Evidence: graphify query 'architecture coupling god nodes'; GRAPH_REPORT communities 15/23/45/67.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Confirm the file map is current, then close via RF-901.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Closed as superseded: the planned splits (RF-102-RF-105) shipped and were verified directly; refactor boundaries are recorded in docs/architecture (backend-roadmap-service-split-plan.md, source-of-truth-rules.md, task-partial-write-api.md).

tasks.title: Split Workspace orchestration into focused containers and hooks
tasks.desc:
Reduce Workspace.tsx from a central conductor into smaller ownership-based pieces.


**Scope**
- Extract view/panel orchestration, participant/team operations, restore/version panel coordination, and task mutation wiring only where it reduces complexity.
- Preserve existing route behavior and localStorage UI state behavior.
- Do not change product behavior.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Workspace becomes easier to scan and does not own unrelated panel internals.
- [ ] Roadmap/Team/Activity/Versions behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] No new monolithic replacement file is introduced.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Workspace decomposition appears implemented: WorkspaceBanners, useAutoSync, useSaveFlow, useTaskMutations, usePhaseMutations, useRoadmapHydration all exist as focused modules.
- Evidence: graphify explain Workspace(); GRAPH_REPORT communities 13/15/22.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: Workspace.tsx (592 lines) delegates to WorkspaceToolbar/WorkspaceBanners/WorkspaceHead/WorkspaceModals plus useWorkspaceViewModel, useAutoSync, useSaveFlow, useTaskMutations, usePhaseMutations, useRoadmapHydration; panels are separate components. Web suite green (303 tests).

tasks.title: Split TaskRow into header, description, metadata, actions, and child sections
tasks.desc:
TaskRow still carries too much UI and state logic. Split it into small components without changing behavior.


**Scope**
- Keep title inline editing and description inline editing intact.
- Keep Estimate, Assignees, and Tags as read-only metadata edited via Edit details.
- Keep Add subtask, Link dependency, claim row, dependencies, subtasks, locks, and read-only states intact.
- Avoid introducing a new all-purpose TaskDetail monolith.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] TaskRow becomes a composition shell with clear subcomponents.
- [ ] Each extracted file has a narrow responsibility.
- [ ] No regression in expanded task behavior.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual check one expanded task.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: TaskRow split appears implemented: TaskRowHeader, TaskDetailMeta, TaskDetailActions, TaskDependencySection, TaskSubtaskSection, TaskClaimRow, SubtaskRow exist.
- Evidence: GRAPH_REPORT communities 29/47/53/58.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: TaskRow composes task-row/ subcomponents (TaskRowHeader, TaskDetailActions, TaskDependencySection, TaskSubtaskSection, TaskClaimRow, TaskLinksSection) plus TaskDetailMeta, TaskDescriptionEditor, SubtaskRow; no TaskDetail monolith was introduced.

tasks.title: Split save, autosync, conflict, and realtime status ownership
tasks.desc:
Make save/sync/conflict behavior easier to maintain before adding GitHub link mutations.


**Scope**
- Separate debounced autosync, explicit save, conflict review, session expiry, and activity refresh responsibilities where practical.
- Keep existing conflict behavior and draft preservation.
- Do not change backend APIs.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Autosync and explicit save flows are easier to test independently.
- [ ] Conflict review still opens for 409 metadata.
- [ ] Offline/session-expired behavior remains unchanged.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual conflict smoke if touched.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Save/sync ownership split appears implemented: useAutoSync, useSaveFlow, SyncStatusIndicator, SyncConflictReviewPanel are separate modules.
- Evidence: GRAPH_REPORT communities 13/63/72.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: useAutoSync and useSaveFlow are separate hooks; 409 conflict metadata opens conflict review (onConflictMetadata -> openConflict, SyncConflictReviewPanel/SyncStatusIndicator); session-expired handling present in useAutoSync and useTaskPatch.

tasks.title: Clarify RoadmapContext and remove legacy combined usage where practical
tasks.desc:
RoadmapContext is already split internally, but many consumers may still use the legacy combined hook.


**Scope**
- Prefer focused hooks for new/refactored components.
- Remove legacy combined usage only where it is safe and improves subscription clarity.
- Keep the legacy hook exported until all major consumers are migrated.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] No component subscribes to more roadmap state than it needs in newly touched areas.
- [ ] No behavior change in hydration or realtime subscription.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Context split appears implemented: RoadmapDataContext, RoadmapLifecycleContext, RoadmapSessionContext exist alongside the legacy combined value.
- Evidence: graphify query 'RoadmapContext'; GRAPH_REPORT community 23.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: RoadmapDataContext/RoadmapSessionContext/RoadmapLifecycleContext exist with focused hooks useRoadmapData/useRoadmapSession/useRoadmapLifecycle; legacy useRoadmap remains exported by design and migrated consumers use the focused hooks.

tasks.title: Modularize workspace CSS by component ownership
tasks.desc:
Reduce CSS sprawl by moving task-detail, toolbar, editor, and panel rules into ownership-based files.


**Scope**
- Do not rename public class names unless every usage is updated in the same slice.
- Prefer additive cleanup with before/after screenshots.
- Keep mobile rules close to their owning component when possible.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] CSS files are easier to navigate.
- [ ] Expanded task detail remains visually unchanged or improved.
- [ ] No broad global resets are introduced.


**Validation**
`pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual desktop/mobile screenshot check.

tasks.title: Write architecture decision records for source-of-truth rules
tasks.desc:
Document the rules that protect RoadForge from turning into an issue tracker or fragile sync layer.


**Scope**
- RoadForge owns roadmap intent, sequencing, and task planning.
- GitHub owns implementation artifacts.
- Imports/exports must stay portable and credential-free.
- Task links are references, not automatic status drivers.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] ADR exists and is referenced by GitHub implementation tasks.
- [ ] Future agents have clear do/don't guidance.


**Validation**
Review only; no runtime validation required.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: docs/architecture/source-of-truth-rules.md exists and is referenced by import/GitHub-link docs (snapshot-canonical doctrine hyperedge).
- Evidence: GRAPH_REPORT hyperedge 'Canonical Snapshot-First Doctrine'.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: docs/architecture/source-of-truth-rules.md exists and is referenced by docs/architecture/overview.md and CLAUDE.md; task-external-links.md applies the doctrine for GitHub link work.

phases.name: Frontend state and task UX consolidation

tasks.title: Finalize compact expanded task detail layout
tasks.desc:
Consolidate the current task detail UX after the metadata regression patch.


**Scope**
- Description inline editing remains available without a noisy text button.
- Estimate, Assignees, and Tags are compact read-only metadata edited through Edit details.
- Action buttons are compact and visually integrated.
- Do one last desktop and mobile visual pass.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] No large full-width metadata boxes.
- [ ] No inline metadata inputs for estimate/assignees/tags.
- [ ] Edit details reliably opens TaskEditForm and preserves lock behavior.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; browser check at desktop and 600px.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Compact detail layout appears largely implemented: TaskDetailMeta, TaskInlineField, TaskDetailActions exist as focused components.
- Evidence: GRAPH_REPORT communities 29/53.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Confirm the final desktop/mobile visual pass happened, then close via RF-901.

tasks.title: Confirm task edit mode coverage after metadata simplification
tasks.desc:
Audit which fields are edited inline versus via Edit details and remove dead inline metadata code.


**Scope**
- Title and description remain inline.
- Estimate, assignees, tags use Edit details.
- Remove unused TaskTagEditor/TaskAssigneeEditor/TagInput props only if no remaining imports need them.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] No dead imports or unused files.
- [ ] TaskEditForm remains the canonical metadata editor.
- [ ] Tests updated if helpers were simplified.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09 (pass 2)**
Verified in RF-901 pass 2: no file in apps/web/src references TaskTagEditor or TaskAssigneeEditor (grep found zero matches); TagInput.tsx and TaskEditForm.tsx exist as the canonical Edit-details editor (apps/web/src/components/roadmap/TaskEditForm.tsx, TagInput.tsx).

tasks.title: Tighten task lock UX around Edit details and description editing
tasks.desc:
Make lock behavior obvious but not noisy.


**Scope**
- Edit details, title edit, description edit, Add subtask, and Link dependency all use the same whole-task lock rules.
- Other-user lock shows a concise hint.
- Idle pause never softlocks the team.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Second user cannot enter edit flows while lock is active.
- [ ] Idle release lets another user eventually edit.
- [ ] Original user's draft is not silently lost.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; two-browser manual QA.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Lock UX plumbing appears implemented: useIdleEditPause, commitTaskField, useInlineTaskEdit and edit-lock hook exist.
- Evidence: GRAPH_REPORT communities 31/40/55.
- Classification: likely
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.

tasks.title: Add focused helper tests for task metadata and description editor behavior
tasks.desc:
Backfill tests for the areas most likely to regress during refactors.


**Scope**
- Pure tests for task metadata display helpers if introduced.
- Description editor behavior tests only if current test setup supports component tests cleanly.
- Avoid brittle screenshot-style tests.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Description draft preservation, dirty cancel, and keyboard save are covered where practical.
- [ ] Metadata simplification does not regress import/export fields.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test` plus full web validation.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09 (pass 2)**
Verified in RF-901 pass 2: apps/web/src/lib/__tests__/task-edit.test.ts covers dirty-draft detection (isTaskEditDraftDirty, isTaskDescriptionDirty preserving exact Markdown/whitespace) and metadata normalization; markdown-description.test.ts covers description parsing; import-merge.test.ts exercises metadata fields (assignees, tags, claim) through import so simplification does not silently drop fields.

tasks.title: Normalize activity labels for task updates and metadata edits
tasks.desc:
Activity should remain useful after the editing model changed.


**Scope**
- Ensure title/description/Edit details changes generate understandable activity.
- Avoid activity spam for no-op saves.
- Do not introduce a new activity model unless necessary.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Activity panel distinguishes meaningful task updates.
- [ ] No duplicate rows for a single user action.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; manual Activity panel check.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09 (pass 2)**
Verified in RF-901 pass 2: apps/web/src/lib/__tests__/activity-changes.test.ts covers getChangedTaskFields (stable field ordering, treats normalized legacy assignment metadata as unchanged so no-op saves don't spam) and mergePendingActivityChange ('merges repeated task edits into one row and retains all changed fields', preventing duplicate rows for one user action).

tasks.title: Clean up local UI state persistence boundaries
tasks.desc:
Ensure per-roadmap UI state stores only UI preferences, not source-of-truth data.


**Scope**
- Expanded task, open phases, dismissed notices belong in UI state.
- Roadmap data, auth, and session state stay separate.
- Parse errors and stale state remain safe.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Storage schema remains backward compatible.
- [ ] Invalid UI state does not crash hydration.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test -- src/lib/__tests__/storage.test.ts && pnpm typecheck`.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09 (pass 2)**
Verified in RF-901 pass 2: apps/web/src/lib/storage.ts defines a dedicated RoadmapUiState (schemaVersion, expandedTaskId, open phases, dismissed notices) kept separate from RoadmapCache/AuthCache; parseRoadmapUiState checks schemaVersion and field types and returns null on any mismatch instead of throwing, so invalid/stale UI state degrades safely.

phases.name: Backend write model, realtime, and data integrity hardening

tasks.title: Design partial task write API shape before implementation
tasks.desc:
Define the partial task write model to reduce full-snapshot conflicts.


**Scope**
- Document endpoint shape, optimistic concurrency, activity metadata, version policy, SSE event behavior, and validation limits.
- Use existing task done/claim routes as the style reference.
- Keep local-first import/export model unchanged.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Design is small enough to implement in reviewable slices.
- [ ] No task field can be silently overwritten by stale clients.


**Validation**
Review only; no runtime validation required.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**

- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: docs/architecture/task-partial-write-api.md documents the endpoint contract, optimistic concurrency, activity, request limits, and the done/claim precedents; implementation landed as reviewable slices (RF-302).

tasks.title: Add task-level PATCH endpoint for title, description, and metadata
tasks.desc:
Implement a focused task PATCH endpoint for scalar task fields and metadata.


**Scope**
- Owner/editor only.
- Accept title, desc, est, assignees, tags plus required last_updated_at.
- Validate with existing limits and normalized helpers.
- Return full RoadmapResponse for frontend compatibility.
- Emit task.updated activity and roadmap.updated SSE.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Partial update touches only provided task fields.
- [ ] Stale last_updated_at returns 409 conflict metadata.
- [ ] Viewer is 403; owner/editor are 200.
- [ ] Tests cover title, desc, est, tags, assignees.


**Validation**
Run full apps/api pytest plus web typecheck.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Endpoint exists: patch_task route, PatchTaskRequest schema, _patch_task_fields_in_snapshot, and test_task_patch.py (35 graph nodes).
- Evidence: graphify path useTaskPatch() -> PatchTaskRequest; communities 0/6/12.
- Classification: confirmed (existence; acceptance-criteria coverage unverified)
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: patch_roadmap_task + PatchTaskRequest with test_task_patch.py covering title/desc/metadata/links, viewer 403, stale 409 structured conflict, unrelated-field preservation, no-op no-write, precise activity fields, SSE publish, and projection sync.

tasks.title: Route synced title and description commits through task PATCH
tasks.desc:
Stop using full roadmap PUT for title and description commits on synced roadmaps.


**Scope**
- Local roadmaps keep current local mutation path.
- Synced roadmaps call task PATCH and apply returned roadmap/updatedAt.
- Conflict feeds existing conflict handling while preserving draft state.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Single title/description edit issues one task PATCH.
- [ ] No full PUT for these edits.
- [ ] Offline/session-expired/conflict paths remain recoverable.


**Validation**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`; two-session conflict QA.


**Audit 2026-07-09 (Graphify)**
- Finding: Frontend wiring exists: useTaskPatch -> patchTask (roadmap-crud.service.ts) with commitTaskField and conflict handlers.
- Evidence: graphify path useTaskPatch() -> PatchTaskRequest (2-hop frontend leg EXTRACTED); community 48.
- Classification: confirmed (existence)
- Action: Verify acceptance criteria via RF-901 close-out sweep, then mark done.


**RF-901 close-out 2026-07-09**
Verified in RF-901 pass: handleUpdateTask routes synced edits through patchSyncedTask with only changed fields (no full PUT); useTaskPatch handles conflict and session-expired paths; local roadmaps keep the local mutation path.

tasks.title: Keep Edit details metadata writes safe with the new PATCH path
tasks.desc:
Route Estimate, Assignees, and Tags through task PATCH when edited via Edit details on synced roadmaps.


**Scope**
- Keep TaskEditForm UI unchanged.
- No reintroduction of inline metadata editing.

Verified in RF-901 pass: Edit details metadata flows through the same changed-fields task PATCH on synced roadmaps; no-op saves return before any write or activity; local roadmaps mutate locally without backend.

tasks.title: Projection-read readiness and drift tests

Make the relational projection trustworthy before considering it for reads.

tasks.title: Redis-backed realtime deployment check

tasks.title: Maximal import/export round-trip fixture
tasks.desc:
Add a compatibility fixture that catches missed task fields.

**Closed 2026-07-09 (pass 3)**
apps/web/src/services/__tests__/roadmap-export-roundtrip.test.ts round-trips a maximal Phase/Task/TagDefinition/TaskExternalLink fixture through exportRoadmap() -> parseImportedRoadmapJson(), asserting every Task field (incl. desc with lists/checkboxes/links, assignees, tags, deps, parentId, claim fields, links) field-by-field, plus a compile-time `Record<keyof Task, true>` canary (and equivalents for TagDefinition/TaskExternalLink) that fails typecheck if a new field is added without updating the fixture. 308/308 web tests, typecheck, and build all green. Found and documented (not fixed, out of scope for this task's field list) a real gap: TagDefinition.createdAt/updatedAt are written on export but dropped on import by tagRegistryFromPayload in roadmap-validation.ts — covered by an `it.fails` canary test so a future fix flips it to a normal assertion.

tasks.title: Confirm request body limits hold without the proxy (RF-AUD-009)
tasks.desc:
**Why**
Self-hosters may run the API without the documented nginx layer; in-app limits must stand alone.


**Finding**
RF-AUD-009 'Request Body Limiting Relies on Proxy' from the audit; BodyLimitMiddleware now exists in-app (community 42), so the finding may already be resolved.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] In-app limit verified against oversized and chunked/no-Content-Length requests.
- [ ] Behavior documented for the no-proxy self-host case.
- [ ] RF-AUD-009 marked resolved or a fix slice defined.


**Validation / gate**

**Risk**
Content-Length-only checks missing chunked bodies - test that path explicitly.


**Evidence**

**Classification**

**RF-901 pass 2 (2026-07-09)**
Still open because: apps/api/src/api/middleware/body_limit.py's own docstring states the limitation is unresolved by design — "if the client omits Content-Length (e.g. chunked encoding), this middleware cannot enforce the limit without consuming the body stream... the reverse proxy should handle that case." apps/api/tests/test_body_limit.py only unit-tests the header-parsing helper (_declared_body_size) against four Content-Length values; no test exercises an actual oversized request through the running middleware, and no test covers a chunked/no-Content-Length request. Acceptance criterion 1 (oversized AND chunked/no-Content-Length) is unmet; RF-AUD-009 is not resolved. A fix (buffered read-and-count fallback for the no-Content-Length case, or an explicit documented self-host requirement to run behind a body-limiting proxy) is out of scope for this docs-only pass.


**Closed 2026-07-09 (pass 3)**
Fixed the gap in-app rather than deferring to the proxy: BodyLimitMiddleware now wraps the ASGI receive callable when Content-Length is absent, tallying cumulative http.request body bytes as the app streams them and aborting with 413 as soon as the running total exceeds REQUEST_BODY_MAX_BYTES, without ever buffering the full body. Declared-Content-Length behavior (413 oversized, 400 malformed) is unchanged. apps/api/tests/test_body_limit.py adds 4 middleware-level tests (oversized declared, malformed declared, oversized streamed/no-Content-Length across multiple messages, under-limit streamed) alongside the 4 existing header-parsing unit tests — 8/8 passing, plus the full apps/api suite (206/206) against real Postgres. docs/backend-api.md's Security notes "Body size limit" bullet documents that both declared and streamed/chunked bodies are enforced in-app for no-proxy self-hosting. RF-AUD-009 resolved.

The second Anvilary app will copy RoadForge somehow; choosing the mechanism deliberately beats discovering it under deadline.


**Finding**
No prior decision exists in the graph. The realistic options are: template repository (fork-and-strip), extracted shared packages, or a hybrid starting from template.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] ADR records the choice with tradeoffs and a revisit trigger.
- [ ] If template: a strip-list of RoadForge-specific modules exists.
- [ ] No implementation in this task.


**Validation / gate**
Review only; no code changes in this task.


**Risk**
Choosing shared packages too early creates a second product (the platform) before the first one has users.


**Evidence**
RF-1201 inventory; graph.json platform-scope node.


**Classification**
likely

tasks.title: Wire a lightweight alpha feedback loop
tasks.desc:
**Why**
An alpha without a feedback channel is a deployment, not a validation. RoadForge is accountless, so feedback must not require accounts or tracking.


**Finding**
GitHub issue templates (bug report, feature request) and a private vulnerability channel already exist; nothing in-app points to them.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] In-app 'Report an issue / give feedback' link to the public tracker (or contact address while the repo is private).
- [ ] Release notes state what feedback is most wanted.
- [ ] No analytics, tracking, or accounts added.


**Validation / gate**
`pnpm --filter web test && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm build`.


**Risk**
Feedback link rot if the repo URL changes at publication - use one redirecting URL.


**Evidence**
graph.json issue template nodes; 'Private security vulnerability disclosure channel' hyperedge.


**Classification**
likely

tasks.title: Review beta criteria and finalize the licensing decision
tasks.desc:
**Why**
After the alpha window, decide what beta means with evidence instead of aspiration, and lock the source-available license before the repo goes public.


**Finding**
'Beta Launch Criteria' exists in the 2026-06-13 audit and a source-available licensing strategy node exists; both predate the alpha and need revalidation against real feedback.


**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Beta criteria reaffirmed or rewritten from alpha feedback.
- [ ] Final license text chosen and consistent with RF-1002's copy fixes.
- [ ] Go/no-go date or trigger for repository publication recorded.


**Validation / gate**
Review only; no code changes in this task.


**Risk**
Letting the license decision slip until publication day - it gates the public repo.


**Evidence**
graph.json 'Beta Launch Criteria' and licensing strategy nodes.


**Classification**
likely
